page: Google Cloud Platform h4d-highmem-192 is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the h4d family. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 1488.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory density of 7.75 GB per vCPU, positioning it for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

This server configuration does not include local NVMe SSD storage or integrated GPU hardware. Storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage, and workloads requiring hardware acceleration are not supported. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The h4d-highmem-192 offers high resource density for workloads requiring large memory footprints without the overhead of local storage or GPU accelerators. This profile is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform h4d-highmem-192 is an x86_64 architecture server within the h4d family, configured with 192 dedicated vCPUs and 1488.0 GB of RAM. This memory-optimized instance provides 7.75 GB of memory per core, making it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, meaning all storage needs must be handled via network-attached volumes. This resource allocation is designed for workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, data warehousing, and heavy enterprise application servers that require substantial memory capacity without the need for local physical disks or graphics processing units.
